scenarios.uncertain_statement: The vendor docs say the rate limit is 100 req/s but I've seen throttling at 80 in our tests.
scenarios.reference_answer: The documented rate limit is 100 req/s, though testing shows throttling at 80. Treat 80 as the safe operating limit until confirmed with the vendor.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Auth tokens seem to expire after about 3600 seconds based on our logs — I haven't checked the spec.
scenarios.reference_answer: Logs suggest a 3600-second token expiry, but this hasn't been verified against the spec. Treat as an estimate.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Someone on the team heard the batch endpoint accepts up to 50 items — not in any docs I've seen.
scenarios.reference_answer: 50 items per batch was reported by a team member but isn't documented. Verify before relying on it.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Sales told us webhooks deliver within 5 seconds, but the contract says best effort only.
scenarios.reference_answer: Sales quoted 5-second webhook delivery, but the contract guarantees only best effort. Don't rely on 5s for time-critical flows.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Pagination cursors appear to expire — we've seen errors after about 10 minutes of inactivity, docs are silent.
scenarios.reference_answer: Testing suggests cursors expire after roughly 10 minutes, but this isn't documented. Implement refresh logic and monitor for cursor-expired errors.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Our account manager said we have 20 concurrent REST connections — the portal shows no limit.
scenarios.reference_answer: An account manager mentioned 20 concurrent connections, but this isn't reflected in the portal or contract. Treat as an informal guideline and verify.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: A Stack Overflow answer from last year says the idempotency key TTL is 24 hours — no official docs.
scenarios.reference_answer: A Stack Overflow answer cited 24h idempotency TTL, but there's no official documentation. Don't rely on this; contact the vendor.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The integration guide mentions 99.95% uptime for short code delivery but our SLA doesn't mention it.
scenarios.reference_answer: The integration guide references 99.95% uptime, but your SLA doesn't include this guarantee. Get it in writing before committing.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Heap is growing at roughly 2MB per 1000 requests — eyeballed from Grafana, not a profiler run.
scenarios.reference_answer: Grafana shows roughly 2MB growth per 1000 requests — this is a visual estimate, not a profiling measurement. Run a heap profiler before acting on this figure.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The race condition seems to trigger at 8+ concurrent writes — but our load tests aren't deterministic.
scenarios.reference_answer: Load tests suggest the race condition triggers at 8+ concurrent writes, but the tests aren't deterministic — the real threshold could be lower.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: I think the N+1 query in the user profile loader is causing the p99 regression — haven't confirmed with tracing.
scenarios.reference_answer: The N+1 query hypothesis is plausible for the p99 regression, but hasn't been confirmed with distributed tracing. Don't revert the deploy until tracing confirms.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We think we lost about 0.3% of writes during the outage — based on log gaps, not a full audit.
scenarios.reference_answer: Log gap analysis suggests ~0.3% write loss, but this is an unaudited estimate. Do a full record reconciliation before reporting to customers.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: At current growth rate, we'll run out of disk in about 6 weeks — assuming linear growth.
scenarios.reference_answer: Linear extrapolation gives ~6 weeks until disk exhaustion, but actual usage may accelerate. Plan remediation within 2-3 weeks.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The 503s seem to correlate with the nightly ETL at 2am — but I only have 3 days of data.
scenarios.reference_answer: The ETL correlation is based on 3 data points — insufficient to establish causation. Capture 2+ weeks before changing the ETL schedule.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Deadlocks occur maybe once every 500 transactions in staging — not sure if this reflects production.
scenarios.reference_answer: 1-in-500 deadlock rate is from staging and may not represent production contention. Monitor with production counters before using this for SLA calculations.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Queue should clear in about 4 hours at normal processing speed — but speed varies under backpressure.
scenarios.reference_answer: Estimated 4-hour recovery assumes stable processing speed. Under backpressure, communicate a 4-8 hour range to stakeholders.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: I think 16 shards will handle 10 million users — but that's back-of-envelope and the distribution might be skewed.
scenarios.reference_answer: 16 shards is a reasonable starting estimate for 10M users, but back-of-envelope with unknown distribution. Model actual user_id distribution before committing.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Based on one week of logs, we project 85% cache hit rate — but we haven't accounted for seasonal spikes.
scenarios.reference_answer: 85% hit rate is projected from one week of logs — seasonal spikes could shift this 10-15 points. Size cache for a 70% floor assumption.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The service mesh vendor claims 0.5ms overhead per hop, but our benchmarks show 1.2ms.
scenarios.reference_answer: Vendor quotes 0.5ms per hop; your environment measured 1.2ms. Budget 1.2ms as a provisional figure, treat as uncertain until more measurements.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: At current ingestion rates, 90-day retention costs roughly $12,000/month — but that assumes current data mix.
scenarios.reference_answer: ~$12,000/month is a rough estimate sensitive to data mix. Recalculate with new data type profiles before budgeting.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We estimate GraphQL will reduce average payload size by 40% — based on analyzing 20 API calls.
scenarios.reference_answer: 40% reduction estimate is from 20 API calls — too small a sample. Validate on 200+ real queries before committing to capacity planning.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: CDN should handle about 80% of requests based on our static-asset ratio — haven't measured actual offload.
scenarios.reference_answer: 80% CDN offload is theoretical from static-asset ratio analysis. Measure from CDN analytics after launch before sizing origin.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Logs are growing at roughly 50GB per day — but we're about to onboard three high-volume services.
scenarios.reference_answer: 50GB/day is pre-onboarding. With three high-volume services, actual growth could be 2-5x. Size log storage for 300GB/day.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We think 3 replicas will maintain HA under peak load — intuition from past projects, not a load test.
scenarios.reference_answer: 3 replicas is intuition-based from past projects, not a load test on this system. Run failure simulation at peak before going to production.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Our legal team believes GDPR deletion must complete within 30 days — haven't got a formal opinion.
scenarios.reference_answer: 30 days aligns with GDPR Article 17, but your legal team's belief isn't a formal opinion. Get legal advice before publishing your SLA.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We believe we have 72 hours to notify under GDPR — but counsel mentioned new AI-system guidance we haven't reviewed.
scenarios.reference_answer: 72 hours is standard GDPR breach notification, but new AI-system guidance may change this. Review before your next DR exercise.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We're estimating 1.2 million PHI records in scope — but some records haven't been classified yet.
scenarios.reference_answer: 1.2M is an undercount — unclassified records must be included. Use the upper-bound figure for the HIPAA risk assessment.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The SOC 2 audit covers 12 months ending March 2026 — but the auditor flagged a 3-week logging gap we haven't resolved.
scenarios.reference_answer: A 3-week logging gap is a control exception — whether it's material depends on the auditor's assessment. Don't assume it's non-material.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We assume our EU DPA covers UK-resident data too — but the solicitor mentioned UK/EU GDPR diverged post-Brexit.
scenarios.reference_answer: Post-Brexit UK GDPR is a separate framework. One DPA may not cover both. Get a formal legal opinion before assuming coverage.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We think contractors need only a basic background check for Level 2 data access — the enterprise contract may require more.
scenarios.reference_answer: Your enterprise contract may require enhanced vetting for Level 2 access. Don't provision until you've reviewed the relevant contract clause.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We plan for 90-day coordinated disclosure — but the researcher said they'll publish at 60 days if unacknowledged.
scenarios.reference_answer: The researcher imposed a 60-day deadline that's shorter than your 90-day policy. Without acknowledgment, they may publish at day 60. Respond formally within 24 hours.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We retain access logs for 1 year per internal policy — but a prospective client asks for 2 years and we haven't checked insurance.
scenarios.reference_answer: 1-year retention per internal policy may be insufficient for enterprise clients and cyber insurance. Review both before committing to the vendor questionnaire.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We estimate Haiku will handle 80% of sub-agent tasks and Opus the rest — based on one demo, not a real distribution.
scenarios.reference_answer: 80/20 split from a single demo is too thin for cost modeling. Profile 100+ representative tasks before committing to this estimate.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We're allocating 8,000 tokens per sub-agent context — a conservative estimate, not based on profiling.
scenarios.reference_answer: 8,000 tokens is a conservative budget, but real tasks vary widely. Profile your task corpus before finalizing — some reasoning chains exceed 15,000 tokens.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Setting a 30-second tool timeout based on our slowest test call — but that was under light load.
scenarios.reference_answer: 30s from light-load testing may be too tight for production. Queries under contention can take 2-5x longer. Consider 60-90s with circuit breaker logic.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We're assuming Agent A verified a fact means Agent B can trust it — but we haven't tested Agent A's verification quality.
scenarios.reference_answer: Transitive trust is only safe if Agent A's verification is audited and reliable. Verify Agent A's output quality independently before propagating trust.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Our guardrails catch about 95% of policy violations in testing — but we used 50 hand-crafted cases, not adversarial inputs.
scenarios.reference_answer: 95% on 50 hand-crafted cases is not a reliable effectiveness estimate. Evaluate on adversarial inputs and production samples before citing this number.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Hot-swapping the model should add under 200ms — from one internal test, not from load conditions.
scenarios.reference_answer: 200ms from a single internal test isn't reliable. Model swap latency depends on infra state and concurrent load. Measure under realistic conditions before publishing an SLA.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Running 10 agents in parallel should cost about 10x a single agent — but that ignores coordination overhead.
scenarios.reference_answer: 10x linear scaling ignores coordination overhead and retry cascades. Real cost can be 12-15x. Benchmark at 5 agents first.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We think the v1 handoff schema is backward-compatible with v2 agents — based on reading the migration guide, not testing.
scenarios.reference_answer: Schema backward-compatibility claims in migration guides aren't always accurate. Do an integration test with real v1 payloads against v2 agents before deploying.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Preliminary data from 12 patients suggests the dosing might need to be 15mg — not yet statistically significant.
scenarios.reference_answer: Preliminary data from 12 patients suggests 15mg dosing, but this isn't statistically significant. Do not use this as a confirmed dosing recommendation.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The response rate in our pilot was approximately 60% — but pilot N=20 and selection criteria differed from the full trial.
scenarios.reference_answer: ~60% response rate from a N=20 pilot with different selection criteria. Not generalisable to the full trial population.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The device manufacturer claims a 98% sensitivity — but that's from their own internal validation, not an independent study.
scenarios.reference_answer: Manufacturer's 98% sensitivity claim is from internal validation, not independently verified. Treat as an optimistic estimate pending independent data.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We think the intervention reduces readmission by about 20% based on 3 months of data — need longer follow-up.
scenarios.reference_answer: A 20% reduction is preliminary from 3 months of data. Longer follow-up is needed before making clinical recommendations.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The clause might be unenforceable under the new regulations — our counsel hasn't reviewed the latest guidance yet.
scenarios.reference_answer: The clause's enforceability is uncertain pending counsel's review of the new guidance. Don't rely on it until that review is complete.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We estimate litigation exposure at roughly $2 million — based on comparable cases, not a formal legal assessment.
scenarios.reference_answer: ~$2M is a rough estimate from comparable cases, not a formal legal assessment. Obtain a proper assessment before disclosing to stakeholders.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The jurisdiction for disputes is probably New York based on the contract draft — but final version hasn't been reviewed.
scenarios.reference_answer: New York jurisdiction is in the contract draft but final terms haven't been reviewed. Don't rely on this until the executed contract is confirmed.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The vendor says the IP licensing covers our use case — but we haven't had our own counsel review the specific terms.
scenarios.reference_answer: Vendor claims the IP licensing covers your use case, but you haven't had independent counsel review the terms. Get your own legal review before proceeding.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We project Q3 revenue at approximately $4.2M — but the model assumes the partnership closes, which is still in negotiation.
scenarios.reference_answer: Q3 revenue projection of ~$4.2M is contingent on the partnership closing, which is still in negotiation. This is a best-case estimate.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Our burn rate is roughly $380K/month based on last quarter — but headcount is increasing next month.
scenarios.reference_answer: ~$380K/month burn is from last quarter and will increase with the headcount change next month. Reforecast with the new hires included.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The analyst report estimates the TAM at $8 billion — but it's a single third-party report, not a consensus figure.
scenarios.reference_answer: The $8B TAM estimate is from a single third-party analyst report, not a consensus figure. Use as an order-of-magnitude reference, not a precise claim.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Early unit economics show a payback period of about 18 months — but that's from our first 50 customers, which may not be representative.
scenarios.reference_answer: 18-month payback from the first 50 customers is preliminary and may not represent your broader customer base. Monitor as cohort data matures.
